issue_title: Some sort of auto cleanup of ThreadAuthenticator
issue_body:
I work on a project that allows a workflow where a user opens jupyter, calls a function, and gets a pandas dataframe representing some data on a server, which they can analyse interactively. I'm currently implementing zmq's elliptic curve crypto for this so that this is secure on a wider range of networks, since we use pickle to serialise the dataframe and send it over zmq.

Because the workflow is interactive, it's important that when the user hits ctrl-D, their jupyter session exits.

I'm currently having trouble with this, here is an example:

```python
import zmq
from zmq.auth.thread import ThreadAuthenticator

class Context(zmq.Context):
    auth = None
    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
        zmq.Context.__init__(self, *args, **kwargs)
        self.auth = ThreadAuthenticator(self)
        self.auth.start()

context = Context()
server = context.socket(zmq.PULL)
port = server.bind_to_random_port('tcp://*')

client = context.socket(zmq.PUSH)
client.connect('tcp://localhost:%d' % port)

for i in range(10):
    msg = b'hello'
    client.send(b'hello')
    assert server.recv() == msg
print('done')

```

In Python 3.6 and pyzmq 16.0.2, when run as python -i example.py, this requires an additional ctrl-C after hitting Ctrl-D to make it exit. In Python 2.7 (also with pyzmq 16.0.2), no amount of Ctrl-C's will make it exit.

One simple suggestion might be to (optionally?) make the thread that the authenticator runs in a daemon thread:

```diff
--- thread_orig.py	2018-03-07 14:58:46.596517503 +1100
+++ thread.py	2018-03-07 15:41:10.776184298 +1100
@@ -164,6 +164,7 @@
         self.pipe.linger = 1
         self.pipe.bind(self.pipe_endpoint)
         self.thread = AuthenticationThread(self.context, self.pipe_endpoint, encoding=self.encoding, log=self.log)
+        self.thread.daemon = True
         self.thread.start()
         # Event.wait:Changed in version 2.7: Previously, the method always returned None.
         if sys.version_info < (2,7):
```

This makes the Python interpreter quit upon ctrl-D for the above example (both Python 2 and Python 3). But I'm not sure if it's strictly correct, so it might not be the right fix. If I understand correctly, it's important that the authenticator sockets remain bound to their endpoints until after all other sockets are done with, otherwise there will be no security, so I'm not sure if simply making it a deamon thread might lead to the situation where your code is briefly accepting any connections at interpreter shutdown.

As for relying on garbage collection, even though `ThreadAuthenticator` has `self.stop()` in its `__del__` method, it's difficult to set up a situation where it is deleted when the associated `Context` is deleted (for example, by subclassing `Context` and making the `ThreadAuthenticator` an instance attribute, as I've done). This is because the thread holds a reference to the `Context` (and also to the `pipe` socket, which itself holds another reference to the `Context`), and thus even if no references remain in the main thread, the `Context` still doesn't get garbage collected. Maybe weak references could be made use of to alleviate this.

Anyway, consider this a feature request for auto cleanup of ThreadAuthenticators such that the above example might work the way I'd ideally like. If I knew how to do it properly this would be a pull request instead, but I don't so I'm asking for help instead. If anyone wants to recommend an approach I'd happily implement it and make a pull request.

issue_title: Allow for timeout and retry configuration of the underlying `requests.Session`
issue_body:
## Environment

cloudflare==2.1.0
requests==2.18.4

## Current behavior

Using this module from inside China means that requests going to the Cloudflare API are quite unreliable. We find our code based on this module behaves consistently when run from outside of China, but in China will often hang for the first request, causing our script to run for a very long time. Since we run this script every minute on our ops server to update DNS records, if this script runs long, we will get an accumulation of instances running the script, which would eventually overload the server.

This one run got stuck for over 8 minutes:
```
^CTraceback (most recent call last):
  File "/srv/managebac/shared/bin/cloudflare_update.py", line 100, in <module>
    cf_zone = cf_get_zone(cf_client, zone_domain)
  File "/srv/managebac/shared/bin/cloudflare_update.py", line 37, in cf_get_zone
    zones = cf_client.zones.get(params={"name": zone_domain})
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/CloudFlare/cloudflare.py", line 618, in get
    params, data)

    params, data, files)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/CloudFlare/cloudflare.py", line 424, in _call
    params, data, files)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/CloudFlare/cloudflare.py", line 319, in _raw
    params, data, files)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/CloudFlare/cloudflare.py", line 188, in _network
    data=data)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/requests/sessions.py", line 521, in get
    return self.request('GET', url, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/requests/sessions.py", line 508, in request
    resp = self.send(prep, **send_kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/requests/sessions.py", line 618, in send
    r = adapter.send(request, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/requests/adapters.py", line 440, in send
    timeout=timeout
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/urllib3/connectionpool.py", line 601, in urlopen
    chunked=chunked)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/urllib3/connectionpool.py", line 346, in _make_request
    self._validate_conn(conn)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/urllib3/connectionpool.py", line 850, in _validate_conn
    conn.connect()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/urllib3/connection.py", line 284, in connect
    conn = self._new_conn()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/urllib3/connection.py", line 141, in _new_conn
    (self.host, self.port), self.timeout, **extra_kw)

KeyboardInterrupt

real	8m9.595s
user	0m0.268s
sys	0m0.032s
```

## Expected behavior

The library should follow the advice from http://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#timeouts and set a default timeout for all requests, and, if possible, make this user-configurable.

> You can tell Requests to stop waiting for a response after a given number of seconds with the timeout parameter. Nearly all production code should use this parameter in nearly all requests. Failure to do so can cause your program to hang indefinitely:

A "nice to have" would be for the library to retry failed requests using urllib's `HTTPAdapter`. See http://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/_modules/requests/adapters/ and https://stackoverflow.com/a/15431343/3409092.

issue_title: S3Transfer.upload_file calls callback twice
issue_body:
As per the [examples in the docs](https://boto3.readthedocs.org/en/latest/reference/customizations/s3.html#usage), passing a callback to `S3Transfer.upload_file` displays the upload progress. However because `botocore.auth.SigV4Auth.payload` consumes the request body this actually happens twice, once for calculating the SHA256 of the body and one more when sending the body. This is somewhat cumbersome to workaround, I did it by adding these lines to the `Progress.__call__` example:

```
            if bytes_amount > 0 and self._seen_so_far == self._size:
                # assume we are now only starting the actual transfer
                self._seen_so_far = 0
```

It would be nice if somehow this could be done a bit better, seems however that botocore has no support for progress reporting and I don't see an obvious way to do it correctly (apart from the present solution).

issue_title: universal wheel is built with python 2.6-only version pinning on python-dateutil
issue_body:
Someone built the universal wheel for 1.10.4 using Python 2.6. This has caused the universal wheel on PyPI to include the following `Requires-Dist: python-dateutil>=2.1,<2.7.0` instead of the more relaxed `Requires-Dist: python-dateutil>=2.1,<3.0`.

```
botocore 1.10.4 has requirement python-dateutil<2.7.0,>=2.1, but you'll have python-dateutil 2.7.2 which is incompatible.
```

The `setup.py` needs to have version pins using environment markers (similar to the already-defined and redundant ordereddict and simplejson deps in the extras) instead of imperatively defined in the code in order for the metadata to get into the wheel correctly.

issue_title: Raise exception if `request.tm` is accessed for request opted out by `activate_hook`
issue_body:
If you use an `activate_hook` to opt certain requests out of the transaction manager tween, it is currently still possible to get a reference to the request transaction manager using `request.tm`. 

This isn't usually a big problem, but if instead of using a threadlocal transaction manager, you are using a per-request tm, say

```python
config.add_settings({
   "tm.manager_hook": lambda request: transaction.TransactionManager(),
})
```

and then registering the session with the transaction manager using `zope.sqlalchemy.register(session, transaction_manager=request.tm)`, then it's possible to end up in a situation where the session is registered with the transaction manager but the transaction manager isn't hooked up to the request lifecycle.

This wouldn't be the end of the world, but it results in references to `id(session)` not being cleaned up from [the `_SESSION_STATE` threadlocal](https://github.com/zopefoundation/zope.sqlalchemy/blob/9b70ff1cdbebb328ed287183112b696c1184ec61/src/zope/sqlalchemy/datamanager.py#L48) in `zope.sqlalchemy`.

In an environment where a new `Session` instance is created for each request, this results in a (small) memory leak. Much more importantly, it is very easy to get into a situation where a brand-new session is created with an `id(session)` that's already in `_SESSION_STATE`, resulting in `zope.sqlalchemy.register` silently failing to register the session with the transaction manager.

Of course, part of the problem here is that of usage: why would you call `zope.sqlalchemy.register(...)` in a request that you opted out of the transaction manager machinery. But I'd argue that this is a bit of a footgun, and `pyramid_tm` could prevent this class of bug entirely if the `tm` request property raised an `AttributeError` (or a custom exception type) when used in the context of a request for which `activate_hook` returned False.

I'm happy to put together a PR to implement this behaviour, but thought I should open this issue for feedback on the idea before I did so. Thoughts?

issue_title: Allow max_chunk_size to be configurable
issue_body:
[Here](https://github.com/gitpython-developers/GitPython/blob/cf8dc259fcc9c1397ea67cec3a6a4cb5816e3e68/git/cmd.py#L659) `max_chunk_size` will usually contain (depending on the platform) `8192`, which is a very high value for a chunk and won't trigger a `.write` (keep reading) until quite some output is printed to stdout. I propose to make that value configurable (maybe via an argument).

My use case:

I have the following class:

	class PipeIO(io.BytesIO):
		def __init__(self, cb):
			io.BytesIO.__init__(self)
			self.updater_cb = cb

		def write(self, b):
			buf = io.BytesIO.getbuffer(self).tobytes()
			progress = re.findall("(?<=\()\d+\/\d+(?=\))", buf.decode("utf-8"))

			try:
				self.updater_cb(progress[-1])
			except:
				pass

			return io.BytesIO.write(self, b)

And I use it like this:

    stdout = PipeIO(self.my_updater_callback)
    self.repo.git.filter_branch("-f", "--env-filter", s, output_stream = stdout)

This will cause my callback to parse the content of the buffer every time [`.write`](https://github.com/gitpython-developers/GitPython/blob/cf8dc259fcc9c1397ea67cec3a6a4cb5816e3e68/git/util.py#L127) is called, which is very useful if I want to provide progress updates while running a long git command (like `filter-branch`).

Possible solution:

Make it possible to call raw commands with one extra argument:

    self.repo.git.filter_branch("-f", "--env-filter", s, output_stream = stdout, max_chunk_size = 32)

issue_title: Implement in API resource updates via their natural ID
issue_body:
`pynsot` has had an issue to add natural ID updates/deletes for awhile. (hostname, cidr, interface name) I think this should be at the API level to make NSoT much easier to use from a programmer's POV.

This is somewhat related to #36

Currently if I want to sync resources from something continuously, I need to implement too much logic to handle creating the resource initially, allowing that to fail, fetching existing resource id, updating via id.

issue_title: How to install from local folder?
issue_body:
We use internal proprietary libraries and we found that:

1. putting `--find-links=requirements/local` int `requirement.in`, 
2. listing  an unfreezed proprietary dependency in a `requirement.in`,
3. dropping a proper package version `whl` file in `requirements/local` project folder,
4. freezing versions in `requirements.txt` file just before release with `pip-compile`,
5. and shipping `requirements/local` folder contents to the server with project.

is the most easy solution for using private libraries. No need to setup private pypi server/source and use that securely.

When dependency has a newer version that we want we just drop this dependency `whl` file new version in the `requirements/local` and remove the old file. No need to update `requirements.in` by hand, update dependency path or whatever. This is also an easy update to be made by scripts, just delete old `requirements/local/dependency-*.whl` and put a new one, no need to parse and replace destination requirement files.

I was wondering how to recreate this with `Pipfile`. I was trying to do it with `[[source]]` but I'm not sure how to point to a project relative `requirements/local` folder in `url` attribute.

Is it doable? Can I somehow recreate `--find-links=requirements/local` effect in Pipfile?

issue_title: Auditwheel stripping rpath for relative paths
issue_body:
Hi

I'm developing a library that has several C++ extensions, some of which are linked internally. For each extension library that has a dependency, I add the relative path (with $ORIGIN) to the linked library, as such:

    runtime_library_dirs.append("\$ORIGIN/%s" % <rel-path-between modA.so and modB.so>)

This gives me .so files with dependencies:

```
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libptf77blas.so.3]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libptcblas.so.3]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libatlas.so.3]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [modA.so]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libstdc++.so.6]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libm.so.6]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libgcc_s.so.1]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libpthread.so.0]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libc.so.6]
 0x000000000000000e (SONAME)             Library soname: [modB.so]
 0x000000000000001d (RUNPATH)            Library runpath: [$ORIGIN/relpath/to/modA]
```


However, when using the auditwheel tool, the runpath is stripped and replaced with $ORIGIN/../../../.libs. As such, my library (here modA.so) can no longer be found:

```
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libptf77blas-536e8f3e.so.3.0]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libptcblas-f940d3f1.so.3.0]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libatlas-dd66cbe8.so.3.0]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [modA.so]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libstdc++.so.6]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libm.so.6]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libgcc_s.so.1]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libpthread.so.0]
 0x0000000000000001 (NEEDED)             Shared library: [libc.so.6]
 0x000000000000000e (SONAME)             Library soname: [modB.so]
 0x000000000000001d (RUNPATH)            Library runpath: [$ORIGIN/../../.libs]
```

It would be really nice if there was a way to have the runpath appended with the path to the grafted libraries, and not replaced. Is there some solution to this? Or, am I missing something?

#!python

    packages=find_packages(..)

on the developer machine will fail to find packages that lack a `__init__.py` file, as is allowed in Python 3.3. However, such packages listed explicitly: packages=['mypkg'] do appear to get included and later installed.

issue_title: `develop` and `install --single-version-externally-managed` are not compatible with each other for namespace packages
issue_body:
Originally reported by: **hongqn (Bitbucket: [hongqn](http://bitbucket.org/hongqn), GitHub: [hongqn](http://github.com/hongqn))**

---

To reproduce this issue, do the following in a virtualenv:
1. prepare source code of two packages with same namespace package `ns`:
   
   ```
   mkdir -p pkg1/ns/pkg1 pkg2/ns/pkg2
   echo "from setuptools import setup, find_packages; setup(name='pkg1', packages=find_packages(), namespace_packages=['ns'])" > pkg1/setup.py
   echo "__import__('pkg_resources').declare_namespace(__name__)" > pkg1/ns/__init__.py
   touch pkg1/ns/pkg1/__init__.py
   echo "from setuptools import setup, find_packages; setup(name='pkg2', packages=find_packages(), namespace_packages=['ns'])" > pkg2/setup.py
   echo "__import__('pkg_resources').declare_namespace(__name__)" > pkg2/ns/__init__.py
   touch pkg2/ns/pkg2/__init__.py
   ```
2. install pkg1 using --single-version-externally-managed
   
   ```
   cd pkg1
   python setup.py install --single-version-externally-managed --record record.txt
   cd ..
   ```
3. install pkg2 using develop
   
   ```
   cd pkg2
   python setup.py develop
   cd ..
   ```
4. pkg1 is accessible
   
   ```
   python -c 'import ns.pkg1'
   ```
5. pkg2 is unexpectedly inaccessible
   
   ```
   python -c 'import ns.pkg2'
   Traceback (most recent call last):
     File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
   ImportError: No module named pkg2
   ```

This was discussed in pip's issue tracker https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/3 years ago (because pip uses `install --single-version-externally-managed` for `pip install` and use `develop` for `pip install --editable`), but they still have not gotten a resolution.

I think this issue should be resolved in setuptools instead of pip.  `install --single-version-externally-managed` creates a `{pkg}-nspkg.pth` in site-packages directory.  It will create a ns module and assign its `__path__` to the ns directory in site-packages.  So `pkg_resources.declare_namespace()` in pkg2's ns/__init__.py has no chance to run.

 I have two proposals to fix it:
1. Fix `install --single-version-exteranlly-managed`.  Modify the `{pkg}-nspkg.pth` file to append a line
   
   ```
   import pkg_resources; pkg_resources.declare_namespace('ns')
   ```
   
   at the end of it.  So it will scan `sys.path` to gather other directories into `ns.__path__`.
   
   A tricky point is that we must ensure the `{pkg}-nspkg.pth` filename is alphabetically larger than `easy-install.pth`, so that we have source code directory installed by `develop` command in `sys.path` when running `declare_namespace`.  Is to rename to `zzz-{pkg}-nspkg.pth` a good idea?
2. Fix `develop` command.  Create a `{pkg}-nspkg.pth` in site-packages directory when a namespace package is installed using `develop` command, like what `install --single-version-externally-managed` does.  The content of the pth file could be:
   
   ```
   /path/to/sourcecode
   import pkg_resources; pkg_resources.declare_namespace('ns')
   ```
   
   The `/path/to/sourcecode` line is duplicated with what is inserted in easy-install.pth.  It is here to make sure `sys.path` contains it before `declare_namespace` runs.  It could be omitted if we have a `zzz-` prefix for the pth filename.

What's the dev team's opinion?  I prefer to the second proposal because it only affects `develop` command which is mostly used in develop environment so it will not affect production systems.

I'd like to contribute code fix if you agree with either one of the proposals.

---
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/pypa/setuptools/issue/250

issue_title: Adding placeholders to a template after objects already saved breaks things in a bad way.
issue_body:
We are using `fluent-contents` (without `fluent-pages`). We are using the "CMS system" style with dynamic layout switching. A particular layout's template initially had only one placeholder defined in it. Objects using this layout have already been saved to the database and it is working as expected.

We need to add a second placeholder to the existing layout's template. When we do, the new placeholder is detected and rendered in the form, but when we save the form, we get a `ValidationError: '' value must be an integer."` originating in `fluent_contents.admin.genericextensions.BaseInitialGenericInlineFormSet._construct_form`.

While digging through the code, we noticed what looks like a related bug in `BaseInitialGenericInlineFormSet.__get_form_instance()`. The code assumes that the number of forms in the formset equals the number of `Placeholder` objects already saved in the database and that their sequence is a match. We fixed that problem with the following code:

```
    def __get_form_instance(self, i):
        instance = None
        try:
            # Editing existing object. Make sure the ID is passed.
            instance = self.get_queryset().filter(slot=self._initial[i]['slot'])[0]
        except IndexError:
            try:
                # Adding new object, pass initial values
                # TODO: initial should be connected to proper instance ordering.
                # currently this works, because the client handles all details for layout switching.
                queryset_count = self.get_queryset().count()
                values = self.__initial_minus_queryset()[i - queryset_count]

                values[self.ct_field.name] = ContentType.objects.get_for_model(self.instance)
                values[self.ct_fk_field.name] = self.instance.pk
                instance = self.model(**values)
                instance.save()
            except IndexError:
                pass

        return instance
```

This code does two things differently:

1) It gets the `Placeholder` by slot name instead of by index, in case they are out of order (e.g. the template is updated and their positions are reversed).

2) It calls `instance.save()` to persist the new `Placeholder` object to the database as soon as it is needed by the frontend, when the change form page is rendered.

This fixed the `ValidationError`, but on save we got a different error, instead...

```
IntegrityError: duplicate key value violates unique constraint "fluent_contents_placeholde_parent_type_id_451c85966d08dedf_uniq"
DETAIL:  Key (parent_type_id, parent_id, slot)=(10, 100607, main) already exists.
```

It seems that `fluent-contents` is trying to create a `Placeholder` that already exists. Perhaps because we already created it earlier in `__get_form_instance()`?

@sjdines and I spent a couple hours trying to debug this, and it seems pretty complex. Perhaps this would be an easy fix for you, @vdboor, or you could shed some light on it for us.

Should we be trying to create and save a `Placeholder` object as soon as the frontend needs one, or only when content items are saved to a placeholder?

Or should we be trying to fix the `ValidationError` another way?

issue_title: subprocess stdout and stderr bytes handling in python 3.*
issue_body:
the `subprocess` module in python 3 returns a *byte sequence* for the stdout and stderr. The problem is that the function `_parse_jobid` and `_parse_njobs` from the ``common_adapter.py`` module, treat the output like a **str** because that is the standard for python 2 but for python 3 those functions fail when they receive an  array of bytes for input:
````python
 return len(output_str.split('\n'))-1
TypeError: a bytes-like object is required, not 'str'
````

issue_title: apply_parallel hints
issue_body: Currently, users need to understand the implementation of whatever algorithm they're trying to parallelize using `apply_parallel`.  We can provide a function, e.g., `apply_parallel_hints`, that takes the exact same input arguments, but then uses dask with varying overlaps on random data until the parallel and non-parallel versions give the same result.  It can also check whether the parallelized version is faster.  All of that is then reported to the user so they may use `apply_parallel` with the correct parameters.

issue_title: RFC formatter: support only jinja formatting + heavy API refactoring (principles)
issue_body:
While this is not the most urgent issue, I'd like a general opinion on if this is the way to go in the future. The RFC would have to be fleshed out to be more specific, so this is just the about agreeing on some principles.

**Problem:**
Currently we support many different formatting engines with lots of legacy code which makes maintenance hard. The current code is mixing responsibilities - e.g. formatting of a record representation, fetching of that record, caching, debug, which also makes maintenance and replacement of individual parts hard. New code written for overlays is in danger of depending too much on legacy code (using e..g existing bfe_elements), thus making transition harder in the future. 

**Proposal:**

- [ ] Support only Jinja based formatting. Meaning bfe elements, XSLT transformation (which anyway requires MARCXML) and bfx will disappear.
- [ ] Rebuild module from scratch with a clean API. I.e. de-scope module to only deal with formatting of a record representation (``format_record`` should not take an. Create separate modules to deal with caching, etc.
- [ ] Decide on responsibilities between records and formatting module (i imagine records is in charge of transformation, while formatter takes any transformation and renders it).
- [ ] Existing data model with format template and output formats will be kept.
- [ ] Build legacy layer that implements most important methods from old api, using the new modules. Likely perhaps even the old engines could be support if specifically enabled.

P.S Don't shoot me :-)

issue_title: BibCirculation: retired CERN members
issue_body:
Originally on 2013-03-21

If `CFG_CERN_SITE=1` (i.e. in CDS), BibCirculation assume the existence of the ccid identifier for borrowers. Additionaly it assumes borrowers are all included in LDAP.

This is actually not true for *retired people*.

In particular, in order to register as borrower a retired person one have to visit by hand the URL:
`/admin/bibcirculation/bibcirculationadmin.py/add_new_borrower_step1`.

There one can enter several details but not ccid.

In order to further introduce the ccid one have to go to the Python prompt and type:

```python
from invenio.dbquery import run_sql
email_of_borrower = "..."
ccid_of_borrower = "..."
run_sql("UPDATE crcBORROWER SET ccid=%s WHERE email=%s", (ccid_of_borrower, email_of_borrower))
```

This kind of sub-optimal. It should be possible for the Librarian to be able through the menus to reach `/admin/bibcirculation/bibcirculationadmin.py/add_new_borrower_step1` and to be able to fill in the `ccid`.

Of course this is a risky operation so enough verbiage should be given in order for the Librarian to be aware of the risk of inserting wrong ccid information for existing users.

issue_title: bibsched should be part of init script
issue_body:
Originally on 2010-12-18

bibsched daemon should be part of the init script like other server application. In this way it could be started automatically upon reboot of a machine and it could halt in a clean way all the running tasks in case of a shutdown.

This would be very useful in case e.g. of unforeseen powercuts.

issue_title: Permalink-like support for URLs referenced in record
issue_body:
Originally on 2011-04-15

It would be great to be able to consider records stored in Invenio as *master resources of information*. 

If a record references an *external URL*, a weak link between the record in Invenio and the external resource is created. What if this resource is moved? The URL in the record must be updated! But what if some service has been built that assumed the URL initially provided by Invenio in the record would have stayed forever? The service would be broken.

To solve this issue, Invenio should be considered as master, to serve URLs, so that if the external URL changes, it would suffice to update an *internal redirection table*, and any external client, would transparently have the update.
----
This could be implemented in the following way:

- we invent a *new subfield* to 8564 tag, that should contain an ID that will be used to build the "permalink". This subfield should be repeatable. Its value **must** be unique WRT the record. Say *$7*
* everytime bibupload is seeing an 8564 tag with URL (or an FFT that will cause the 8564 tag to be automatically created), if it is missing the *$7* subfield will automatically allocate it (this ID will be uniquely generated after properties of the URL)
* a new URL handler should be added that would handle URLs such as:
```
http://<server>/record/<recid>/permalink/<ID>
```
such handle should check for authorization to access *<recid>* and should then check within the record what *<ID>* resolves to and should redirect the user using via an HTTP 307 (Temporary Redirect) (see also <http://www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc2616/rfc2616-sec10.html>)
* format elements that were previously delegated to display links to the external URLs should instead provide the permalink.
* if the *$7* is missing upon formatting of the record, the original URL should be provided as compatibility.
* if the external URL changes and an update to the record in Invenio is necessary, one have simply to update (e.g. via BibEdit) the $u field for the URL, without touching the *$7*. 
* additionaly we might decide that any bibupload that append a URL, whose field is specifying an existing *$7*, will erase the old field and will become the new URL.
* if a URL is added without specifying a *$7* it would automatically receive one from BibUpload. However if then it becomes clear that this URL should be the new reference for a resource that was previously already specified, the *$7* of the old URL will be added to the new one (so the URL will be resolved by two permalinks, and that's why *$7* should be repeatable).

----

At CERN this might be useful, for example, to implement a more robust integration with *MediArchive*. CDS would be the master reference for MediaArchive URLs, and if an URL on MediaArchive would change, only the record in CDS would need to be updated (any service using CDS as master won't be affected).

issue_title: BibIndex: optimise term reading phase
issue_body:
Originally on 2013-04-22

Currently, when an index is processed, WordTable is reading values from the database three times: once for words, once for pairs, once for phrases.  With the advancement of index centralisations (see #852), it would be ideal to refactor WordTable into IndexTable so that it would read term values only once and then launch three tokenisations (for words, pairs, phrases).

Here is example of current status: indexing 50 records, see 3 calls for
add_recID_range(), and see 150 calls to get_fields().  This is not necessary.  If we eliminate this, the expected speed up will not be much though, about 10% perhaps.  Still, could help in some cases, like when BibAuthorID is contacted.

issue_title: Interface comparison with emtpy __name__ reports incorrect equality
issue_body:
When comparing two different interfaces with the same, empty name they are reported to be equal:

zope.interface version 4.1.3:
```
>>> from zope.interface import Interface
>>> class A(Interface): pass
... 
>>> class B(Interface): pass
... 
>>> A == B  # correctly repots unquality
False

>>> A.__name__ = ''
>>> B.__name__ = ''
>>> A == B  # equality looks incorrect to me.
True
```

With zope.interface 3.6.7 the last test returns ``False``, so they are treated as unequal.

Also, the ``zope.interface.interface.InterfaceClass.__cmp`` function comment says ( https://github.com/zopefoundation/zope.interface/blob/master/src/zope/interface/interface.py#L490 )
```
        # Yes, I did mean to name this __cmp, rather than __cmp__.
        # It is a private method used by __lt__ and __gt__.
        # I don't want to override __eq__ because I want the default
        # __eq__, which is really fast.
```
But the ``__eq__`` function was added with https://github.com/zopefoundation/zope.interface/commit/c7a10ce52ad99fb6528416678fc057e5338d3e2b -- the comment should be changed.

issue_body: Currently receiving on IPv6 requires binding to an IPv6 address. There should be something like add_udp6_reader that will force IPv6. Maybe also support v4-mapped as well so that one socket can receive on either IPv4 or IPv6 on OSes that support it (I think OS X does not though).

issue_title: Mutating operations incorrectly clear expiry
issue_body:
A number of operations modify an existing value, then assign back to `self._db`, which has the side-effect of clearing the expiry time. According to the redis documentation, only operations that completely replace a key (rather than modifying it) clear the expiry. I haven't written any tests yet, but looking at the code, it is probably at least
- decr, incr, incrby, incrbyfloat
- setbit
- ltrim
- rpoplpush

Some other operations where I'm not sure what redis will do
- getset
- rename, renamenx

issue_title: Incorrect split calculation in yahoo-actions
issue_body:
When yahoo-actions is used to fetch split information then the resulting value is either 0 or raises ZeroDivisionError. 

YahooActionReader tries to eval the returned string then take the reciprocal of the evaluated statement: 
https://github.com/pydata/pandas-datareader/blob/331e3895c51326e87e69325e1a14f1e350cb832e/pandas_datareader/yahoo/actions.py#L35

If the split results reduced number of stocks then the eval'd value will be correct, but the reciprocal calculation will result 0 as integers are used by the operation:
```
In [24]: 1/eval('2/1')
Out[24]: 0
```
This issue could be fixed by specifying 1.0 in the numerator.

If the split results increased number of stocks then the `eval()` will yield a zero, which is used in the denominator causing ZeroDivisionError:
```
In [26]: 1/eval('1/2')
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
ZeroDivisionError                         Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-26-dc71badc23e7> in <module>()
----> 1 1/eval('1/2')

ZeroDivisionError: integer division or modulo by zero
```
One possible fix could be to parse the string, convert the numerator and denominator to float then calculate the proper split value.


Env: pandas-datareader==0.5.0, Python 2.7

issue_title: Add loadUI functionality
issue_body:
When using `.ui` files to build an interface graphically with QtDesigner, it is possible to load the interface directly from the `.ui` file rather than having to transform it onto python then import it. This is already integrated into [PyQt4](http://pyqt.sourceforge.net/Docs/PyQt4) and [PyQt5](http://pyqt.sourceforge.net/Docs/PyQt5/designer.html#PyQt5.uic.loadUi), and relatively easy to implement in PySide as seen in [python_qt_binding](https://github.com/ros-visualization/python_qt_binding/blob/groovy-devel/src/python_qt_binding/binding_helper.py#L196), the concurrent that doesn't support Qt5.

I know it's not the workflow used in spyder, but it would be a great utility for some projects (including mine).

issue_title: Integration with pyfolio and Quantopian's new Risk Model
issue_body:
I have been thinking about the nice "Alpha decomposition" #180 feature. The information it provides is actually a small part of what already available in pyfolio  and the Quantopian's new Risk Model. On one side we cannot replicate all the information provided by those two tools but on the other side it would be great to have all that analysis without having to build an algorithm and run a backtest, something that could be integrated into Alphalens.

Then, why don't we create a function in Alphalens that builds the input required by  pyfolio  and the Quantopian's new Risk Model? Alphalens already simulates the cumulative returns of a portfolio weighted by factor values, so we only need to format those information in a way that is compatible with the other two tools. That would be a pure theoretical analysis, but expect for commissions and slippage the results would be realistic.

I haven't looked at pyfolio in details so I don't know the details of the input, but if @twiecki can help me with those details I can work on this feature and the same for  Quantopian's new Risk Mode (I don't know if that is part of pyfolio or a separate project).

issue_title: Add Differential Chromatic Refraction (DCR) as a surface_op
issue_body:
A relatively quick way to incorporate DCR (when using photon-shooting) is to add it as a surface operation.  The way this would work is: 

• Assign wavelengths to photons if not already present.
• For each photon, compute a displacement that depends on the wavelength.  (The displacement also depends on pressure, temperature, and water vapor pressure which should be constant for a given exposure, and airmass which is nearly constant, though I suppose we could use the actual object-dependent airmass if there's an easy way to access that).  Also, I think we'd want to use a _relative_ displacement; i.e., look at the difference in chromatic refraction between this photon's wavelength and some fiducial wavelength.
• Figure out which direction to apply the displacement.  This is the part that I think will be the trickiest to get right. (As in, don't make any minus sign errors).
• Apply displacement for each photon.

There are already tools in dcr.py for much of the above.

I'd like to have this available for LSST DESC DC2.  I probably won't attempt to tackle it before getting the atmospheric PSF finished though, so if anyone else wants to give this a go first, please go ahead (but let me know).

Idea is, having just one css class to throw icon into :before/:after such as ".fa_b-eye" or ".fa_a-eye" which you can then work with

Why?

lets say you have 2 buttons, one button need icon on left, other button icon on right side (typically  arrows "< prev | next >" in pagebar)

issue_title: Icon request: icon-plain-language
issue_body:
I would like to request an icon that represents areas of websites (text), which are written in (simple) plain language and are therefore are easily to understand for people with handicaps. In germany we call it "Leichte Sprache" which translates to "easy (German) language [language used in a simple manner]". 

There is an inofficially icon provided by a inclusion europe network for print products which dosn't transfer that well into (digital) web and certainly isn't created by a professionel designer as most of the work is done on a volunteer basis: https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leichte_Sprache#/media/File:Leichte_sprache_logo.svg 

Another more professionel example: https://www.gruenderwoche.de/assets/bilder/90x62/leichte_sprache_icon_90__62.jpeg

We would like to use this icon to highlight those areas of our websites which provide content in plain language fo people with handicaps. 

It would be great if Font-Awesome could support our growing community by providing an icon-plain-language.

Thank you in advance!
Jan

issue_title: Duplicate declaration
issue_body:
### Describe the problem

I was getting 'duplicate declaration' error when importing two of the same icon names from 'regular' and 'solid'

### What did you expect?

I expected to find some documentation about fixing the issue

### What version and implementation are you using?

Version: any
Browser and version: nuxt/firefox 65/chrome

- [x] SVG with JS
- [ ] Web Fonts with CSS
- [ ] SVG Sprites
- [ ] On the Desktop

### Reproducible test case
```
import {library, dom} from '@fortawesome/fontawesome-svg-core';
import {faStar} from '@fortawesome/fontawesome-pro-solid';
import {faStar} from '@fortawesome/fontawesome-pro-regular';
library.add({faStar})
```

This is how someone who works more than eight hours a day gets to this point. I didn't see it documented anywhere other than looking up `import` from MDN:

```
import {faStar as faStarReg} from '@fortawesome/fontawesome-pro-regular';
```

The `as` syntax is really useful here and I don't think its widely used/known in this particular part of the world. It could be helpful to add a note in the documentation about it so others learn how to gracefully use different styles of icons with the same name.

issue_title: FA5: NPM packages unusable without module loader (free and pro)
issue_body: I’ve scoured the documentation and source code for hours but can find no way of getting the NPM versions of FA v5 (free and pro) to work in the browser. 😞 Is there any way to use the packages _without_ using a module loader? The packages are UMD modules and the examples in the docs suggest it [should be doable](https://fontawesome.com/how-to-use/font-awesome-api#dom-i2svg) but I’ve had no luck. All I'm trying to do is implement the SVG+Javascript usage but using the NPM packages. This seems like such a basic use case that I can't help but think I must be doing something wrong: ```html ``` The files in the v5 zip download work great, but oddly enough they seem to be different than the files in the packages. Specifically, `index.js` (from the package) is missing all the `bootstrap()` code that makes `fontawesome.js` (from the download) work—and both files claim to be v5.0.1. Is there any way to use Font Awesome v5 without using a module loader or checking the downloaded files into source control? A example project demonstrating the issue: [font-awesome-npm-usage.zip](https://github.com/FortAwesome/Font-Awesome/files/1557488/font-awesome-npm-usage.zip)

issue_title: Removing invalid parameter for timezone and datestyle
issue_body:
Hello,

When you configure client settings in postgresql.conf like `timezone` and `datestyle` in the postgresql.parameters' section of patroni, you end up with the following warnings at reload:
```
WARNING: Removing invalid parameter `timezone` from postgresql.parameters
WARNING: Removing invalid parameter `datestyle` from postgresql.parameters
```
The message is thrown because they are not present in the `pg_settings` table, because they are client settings but they are also valid postgresql.conf settings.

In practice, those settings are written in local files and PostgreSQL load them perfectly but the message could be misleading. Can we safely ignore the two warnings?

issue_title: [WIP] Corner cases to address
issue_body:
Discuss the tricky problems the exporter needs to address. They can be addressed by "doing the right thing", documenting the shortcomings, and/or letting the user configure the output they want.

- [x] Custom attributes (not `data-*` attributes but invalid ones like `*ngFor`) – ok with html5lib
- [x] Order in which ranges are applied (`em` in `strong` or `strong` in `em`)
  - Stable (alphabetical) order for now, but not configurable.
- [x] Order in which attributes are rendered can be different between Python versions - use something like `from collections import OrderedDict`?
  - html5lib inserts attributes in alphabetical order
- [x] Nested blocks where nesting jumps one level of depth (depth = 1, then depth = 3)
  - Not supported for now
- [x] `unstyled` blocks without text, should they render as empty `p` tags? `br`?
  - Empty elements for now.

issue_body:
guake-0.6.0-1.fc22

Truncated backtrace:
prefs.py:597:on_palette_name_changed:IndexError: list index out of range

Traceback (most recent call last):
```
  File "/usr/lib64/python2.7/site-packages/guake/prefs.py", line 597, in on_palette_name_changed
    PALETTES[palette_index])
IndexError: list index out of range
```

Local variables in innermost frame:
palette_index: 9
self: PrefsDialog(path="/usr/share/guake/prefs.glade", root="config_window")
combo: <gtk.ComboBox object at 0x7f201d2a2780 (GtkComboBox at 0x1d8d3d0)>

I guess this is related to the different palette introduced in 0.6+, are we careful with how we set them?

Maybe we should use ``PALETTES.get(palette_index, <default>)``

issue_title: Feature request - alert sound in guake .
issue_body:
In guake I cannot hear an alert sound which I can hear in
gnome-terminal or chromium. The test-case is go to either the location
bar in chromium and hit backspace (each time the alert sound will be
played). Same or similar experience can be shared in gnome-terminal.
You hit backspace at the $ prompt itself and the alert sound will be
played.This can also be seen in editors like leafpad when they reach
the end or beginning of the document and go no further. This is in
gnome-flashback.

Having such alert sounds helps whenever you are absent-minded or feeling sleepy and working on the terminal.

issue_title: Running make instal-system as non root user
issue_body:
I using `make install INSTALL=*** PREFIX=***` to build packages for archlinux as none root user which works perfect with one little problem: 

https://github.com/Guake/guake/blob/41c5b8b408b0360483f2e467f616f88a534acf83/Makefile#L44

needs root and fails if I try to run it. Would it be ok to replace this line with something like:

    @update-desktop-database || echo "Could not run update-desktop-database, are you root?"
Or should make install-system only be used to install guake in /usr/...?

This would also be very useful if one would like to install guake on a system where he/she has no root access. (eg for installing guake in ~/.local/...)

issue_title: [gtk3] Incr/Decr Transparency shortcut conflicts with VTE's scrolling
issue_body:
The default shortcut for Increase/Decrease shortcuts are Control+Shift+Up/Down.

VTE uses these keypresses (built-in) for scrolling vertically by a single line, which many people might find useful. Due to Guake grabbing these key combos, they don't reach VTE now.

Suggestion: maybe change these to Ctrl+Shift+Left/Right.

(Nit: Since the feature's name talks about "transparency" rather than "opacity", the "reverse" setting seems a bit more intuitive to me. That is, Ctrl+Shift+Left decreases opacity, aka increases transparency, and C+S+Right decreases transparency. But maybe it's just me.)

issue_title: Make git setup clearer
issue_body:
As discussed in https://github.com/openfisca/country-template/pull/1#issuecomment-284639239:

I don't really see the point of `init + pull` vs `clone`, though.

I guess it's because you don't want `origin` to be set. If that's the case, I would find the intention clearer if you did a `clone` and a `remote remove`.

Another option, which would sound as legit (if not more) to me, would be to download a dump and `git init` from it.

issue_title: API installation fails
issue_body:
In Mes-Aides-UI, I wanted to depend on `openfisca_france[api]` to delegate version upgrades to `openfisca_france`.

I [tried that](https://circleci.com/gh/sgmap/mes-aides-ui/1725#artifacts/containers/0) and got [an error](https://1725-18399519-gh.circle-artifacts.com/0/tmp/circle-artifacts.w2gX7dm/stderr_npm_16211.txt) because `OpenFisca-Parsers` was not installed.

I tried to (update openfisca_france[api])[https://circleci.com/gh/sgmap/mes-aides-ui/1729] to require `OpenFisca-Core[parser]` but that failed too.

I finally (indicated `OpenFisca-Parsers >= 1.0.2, < 2.0`)[https://github.com/guillett/openfisca-france/commit/b76c64e83d1803f16756268737934065f1045e54] as an explicit dependency of `openfisca_france[api]` and [that worked](https://circleci.com/gh/sgmap/mes-aides-ui/1730).

It is not completely satisfying as that requirement needs to be kept in sync between OpenFisca-Core and OpenFisca-France but it makes things easier for new-comers.

I've also done the following experiment on a brand new virtualenv and OpenFisca-Parsers does not get installed.

issue_title: Add support for channel bindings
issue_body:
Related to https://github.com/requests/requests-kerberos/pull/92 and https://github.com/apple/ccs-pykerberos/pull/55. We should attempt to add channel bindings support to WinKerberos.

Microsoft doesn't document support for channel bindings in [InitializeSecurityContext](https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa375507(v=vs.85).aspx), but they do document it for [AcceptSecurityContext](https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa374704(v=vs.85).aspx). I'm guessing / hoping the missing docs for InitializeSecurityContext are an oversite. It looks like we can add a [SecBuffer](https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa379814(v=vs.85).aspx) with BufferType SECBUFFER_CHANNEL_BINDINGS to the [SecBufferDesc instance here](https://github.com/mongodb-labs/winkerberos/blob/0.6.0/src/kerberos_sspi.c#L258). There is an example of someone doing this with NTLM and PyWin32 [here](https://github.com/brandond/requests-negotiate-sspi/blob/0.3.0/requests_negotiate_sspi/requests_negotiate_sspi.py#L65-L75).

The SEC_CHANNEL_BINDINGS struct is [documented here](https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/dd919963(v=vs.85).aspx). It's not clear to me if SSPI equivalents of constants like GSS_C_AF_UNSPEC are actually defined somewhere, or if we can just define them ourselves starting with the value 0.

It's also unclear to me if the current test suite (which uses MongoDB for all its tests) can actually test this feature.

issue_title: Request size is encoded as variable-length MP_UINT
issue_body:
The protocol specification at http://tarantool.org/doc/box-protocol.html mandates a fixed 5-byte MP_UINT value for the BODY + HEADER SIZE field in a message header. That's how responses are encoded by the Tarantool server and requests are encoded by other drivers (I checked tarantool-php).

tarantool-python, however, encodes this field as a variable-length MP_UINT, so it can take 1 to 5 bytes, depending on the request size. The server handles it correctly, it does not enforce the fixed 5-byte field length and accepts an arbitrary MP_UINT. tarantool-python expects a fixed 5-byte field in responses (from Connection._read_response):

```
        # Read packet length
        length = msgpack.unpackb(self._recv(5))
        # Read the packet
        return self._recv(length)
```

Even though it works when talking to the server (because it always replies with a fixed 5-bytes size values), it's still a violation of the protocol spec. For example, if a proxy wants to reply to a PING request by echoing PING packet data in response (which would be correct assuming clients encode PING requests correctly), tarantool-python does not accept such a response. In other words, it sends garbage out, but expects valid replies back in.

issue_title: Warnings during build, including minor bug in examples/segrnn-sup.cc
Training with autobatching seems to be slower than before
issue_body:
I suspect that training with autobatching got a bit slower after some recent commit. 
I compared some examples (train_rnn-autobatch in examples, bilstm-tagger-withchar-bulk, treenn-bulk and rnnlm-batch in neulab/dynet-benchmark) between the latest (a69c515) and an old (a3782a1) commit, and found that old ones were 1.4 ~ 1.8 times faster in all of them. 
I chose a3782a1 for comparison because I remembered it was faster, and I don't think it is _the_ last commit without this speed issue.

issue_title: test_write_ppi_arm_time_vars unit tests depends on locale setting
issue_body: The test_write_ppi_arm_time_vars unit test in `test_cfradial.py` depends on the currently locale setting producing the string `May` for the 5th month in 2011.  This condition is not meet for many locales (for example de_DE) and results in the unit test failing.  Only the non-locale depending portions of the base_time.string attribute should be tested.

issue_title: Check PEP8 compliance in CI
issue_body:
While it is wanted that every commit is checked for PEP8 compliance before submitting a PR, we all know that sometimes things slip under the radar. 

So I propose to add a PEP8 checker (eg. flake8 or similar) to the CI (travis) to check for PEP8 compliance directly after the install section and before testing section. With this applied every PR is tested and fails if the PEP8 compliance isn't met. Besides that, this lowers the burden for reviewers.

issue_title: Remove datastore.Dataset class
issue_body:
The replacement will be handled by a string when dataset ID is explicitly needed (which will only be for power users). Some functionality will be moved to `key.Key` and the rest to the `connection` module as free functions. In order to make use easier, they will also have versions in `__init__` which can use a default / implicit connection.

Steps to complete:

- [x] Make `set_default_connection` similar to [`set_default_dataset`][1] (#478)
- [x] Remove ~~`query()`,~~ `entity()`, and `transaction()` methods from `Dataset`, replace their use elsewhere. (#479)
- [x] Move `Dataset.get_entity` to `Key` (will impact elsewhere, e.g. `Entity.reload`) (#480)
- [x] Move ~~`save()` and `delete()` methods~~ `delete()` method from `Entity` to `Key` (#481)
- [x] Move `allocate_ids()` method from `Dataset` to ~~`connection`~~ `__init__` module as free function (#482)
- [x] Move `get_entities()` method from `Dataset` to ~~`connection`~~ `__init__` module as free function (#483)

issue_title: Datastore: querying in imported file at module scope fails with 401 Deadline Exceeded error
issue_labels: [{'id': 77676109, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20datastore', 'name': 'api: datastore', 'color': 'f8f8f8', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604243, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p2', 'name': 'priority: p2', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 663697852, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/status:%20investigating', 'name': 'status: investigating', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438580, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20bug', 'name': 'type: bug', 'color': 'd93f0b', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
This error happens when executing a datastore query at module scope **triggered by importing the module into another script.** To reproduce make two files in the same directory together with a JSON service account credentials file. Notice where the datastore specific variables need to be set for the example to work.

`test.py`:

```python
from main import main

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
```

`main.py`:
```python
import os

from google.cloud import datastore

CREDENTIALS = 'YOUR-JSON-FILENAME-HERE'
PROJECT = 'YOUR-PROJECT-NAME-HERE'
NAMESPACE = 'YOUR-NAMESPACE-HERE'
KIND = 'YOUR-KIND-HERE'

PROJECT_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.realpath(__file__))
os.environ['GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS'] = \
    os.path.join(PROJECT_DIR, CREDENTIALS)
ds_client = datastore.Client(project=PROJECT, namespace=NAMESPACE)

keys = []
query = ds_client.query(kind=KIND)
for entity in query.fetch():
    keys.append(entity.key.name)


def main():
    print('\n'.join(keys))
```

This will *always* fail with the following stacktrace:

```
File "test.py", line 1, in <module>
    from main import main
  File "/home/user/src/consult/test/main.py", line 11, in <module>
    for entity in query.fetch():
  File "/home/user/src/consult/test/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/api_core/page_iterator.py", line 186, in _items_iter
    for page in self._page_iter(increment=False):
  File "/home/user/src/consult/test/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/api_core/page_iterator.py", line 217, in _page_iter
    page = self._next_page()
  File "/home/user/src/consult/test/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/cloud/datastore/query.py", line 498, in _next_page
    query=query_pb,
  File "/home/user/src/consult/test/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/cloud/datastore/_gax.py", line 134, in run_query
    return super(GAPICDatastoreAPI, self).run_query(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/contextlib.py", line 35, in __exit__
    self.gen.throw(type, value, traceback)
  File "/home/user/src/consult/test/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/cloud/datastore/_gax.py", line 82, in _catch_remap_gax_error
    six.reraise(error_class, new_exc, sys.exc_info()[2])
  File "/home/user/src/consult/test/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/cloud/datastore/_gax.py", line 74, in _catch_remap_gax_error
    yield
  File "/home/user/src/consult/test/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/cloud/datastore/_gax.py", line 134, in run_query
    return super(GAPICDatastoreAPI, self).run_query(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/home/user/src/consult/test/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/cloud/gapic/datastore/v1/datastore_client.py", line 258, in run_query
    return self._run_query(request, options)
  File "/home/user/src/consult/test/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/gax/api_callable.py", line 452, in inner
    return api_caller(api_call, this_settings, request)
  File "/home/user/src/consult/test/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/gax/api_callable.py", line 438, in base_caller
    return api_call(*args)
  File "/home/user/src/consult/test/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/gax/api_callable.py", line 376, in inner
    return a_func(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/home/user/src/consult/test/venv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/google/gax/retry.py", line 127, in inner
    ' classified as transient', exception)
google.api_core.exceptions.Unauthorized: 401 Deadline Exceeded
```

The error goes away if:
1. the query is moved inside of the `main()` function or
2. the code in `test.py` is merged into the bottom of `main.py`

Now, perhaps it is sloppy to run a query at module scope, but I think this should at least be documented here. In my case, the query was buried deep in functions, but the initial call was made at module scope.

**OS type and version**

Ubuntu 17.04

**Python version and virtual environment information `python --version`**

Python 2.7.13

**google-cloud-python version `pip show google-cloud`, `pip show google-<service>` or `pip freeze`**

google-cloud-datastore == 1.4.0

issue_title: pylint and Visual Studio Code autocomplete does not see "google/cloud/*" modules
issue_body:
Dear All,

1. Windows 10

1. Python 2.7.13

1. `pip show google-cloud` output:
Name: google-cloud
Version: 0.26.1
Summary: API Client library for Google Clou
Home-page: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPl
Author: Google Cloud Platform
Author-email: jjg+google-cloud-python@googl
License: Apache 2.0
Location: c:\bin\python27\lib\site-packages

pylint and Visual Studio Code autocomplete does not see `google/cloud/*` modules.
The reason is both of them don't understand that `<python>\Lib\site-packages\google\cloud` is path to modules.
The fix is very easy: create empty `__init__.py` files inside `<python>\Lib\site-packages\google` and `<python>\Lib\site-packages\google\cloud` folders during installation by `pip`.

Thanks

issue_title: Support of SQLAlchemy for Google Big Table
issue_body:
It would be very useful to have Google Big Table supporting 
Python SQL Alchemy.

It seems Cloud MySQL is fine since SQL Alchemy supports it.
However for NoSQL tables, it seems there is not yet support from Google Big Table.

It will really help to develop new applications from Big Table.

        query = ds_client.query(kind=RepeatingJob.KIND)

    from google.cloud import datastore

}
```

issue_title: Translate: fails with large text (errors 411 and 413)
issue_labels: [{'id': 370597684, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20translate', 'name': 'api: translate', 'color': 'f8f8f8', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604243, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p2', 'name': 'priority: p2', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438580, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20bug', 'name': 'type: bug', 'color': 'd93f0b', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
1. Specify the API at the beginning of the title (for example, "BigQuery: ...") General, Core, and Other are also allowed as types Translate 2. OS type and version Linux (Ubuntu 17.10) 3. Python version and virtual environment information `python --version` https://bugs.debian.org/cgi-bin/bugreport.cgi?bug=276655 4. google-cloud-python version `pip show google-cloud`, `pip show google-` or `pip freeze` google-cloud-translate==1.3.0 5. Stacktrace if available Here's what comes back from the API (apologies for the lack of formatting) Error 411 (Length Required)!!1

411. That’s an error.

POST requests require a Content-length header. That’s all we know. 6. Steps to reproduce See code example 7. Code examplepython ``` # -*- coding: utf-8 -*- import os from google.cloud import translate os.environ['GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS'] = "/path/to/credentials.json" translate_client = translate.Client() text_to_translate = ''' 總統：企業加薪 政府給獎勵 〔記者蘇永耀、陳心瑜／綜合報導〕將解決年輕人低薪問題列為今年最重要工作的蔡英文總統，她昨日指出，多年來二十二Ｋ的低薪現象，讓年輕人覺得「有沒有就業都一樣」，也影響他們積極投入就業市場的態度；今年政府將致力解決低薪，若企業的薪資結構有利於年輕人，可能參照國外作法，包括給予津貼、或企業參與政府標案時，給予一些點數上的加權等方式獎勵。 總統上週五與媒體年終茶敘時說，行政團隊今年最重要任務，就是全力改善年輕人的低薪問題；上週六總統還請幕僚到幫員工加薪的「摩斯漢堡」吃早餐。相關人士說，總統這項動作獲得企業回響，府方正考慮安排總統走訪這些企業，給予打氣及肯定。 要求民進黨智庫 研擬對策 在元旦升旗典禮後，蔡總統赴五股賀聖宮參加安座大典，她致詞表示，今年的目標是「解決低薪」，讓年輕人看得到未來，希望提高民眾的所得，讓所有人民都能過好生活。 總統在回應媒體詢問時也說，已要求民進黨中央、智庫審酌當前經濟發展趨勢，研擬解決青年及弱勢問題的對策、論述。 蔡總統表示，舉例來說，一個年輕人如果月入二十二Ｋ，父母又不在台北，每個月扣掉一萬元房租，剩下的錢能做什麼運用？就算住家裡，這樣的薪水很難買房，也就失去離家單飛機會，久而久之會讓他認為，有沒有這份工作，對他的生活沒什麼影響。 解決低薪 讓青年看到未來 總統說，相信現在很多人家裡，都有一個打不定主意要做什麼的小孩，因為兩、三萬元的月薪，會讓年輕人對未來感到焦慮；如果薪水夠好，即便工作再有挑戰性，他們也願意做。 至於如何解決這個問題，總統說，這與產業結構有關，還是要促成產業轉型，同時獎勵願意為年輕人加薪的企業。她也說，政府希望能將人力導入長照等需求大的行業，並為這些從業者加薪，她期待這樣能帶動民間企業的人力需求。 蔡總統也認為，學生最好大三、大四就能和社會接軌，而且不是做一般的臨時工作，比較好的模式是，學校教育與職業間有足夠彈性，年輕人就學後出來工作，有機會再回去唸書，希望未來教育體制提供這樣的彈性。 蔡英文總統昨日指出，今年政府將致力解決低薪，若企業的薪資結構有利於年輕人，可能參照國外作法，包括給予津貼、或企業參與政府標案時，給予一些點數上的加權等方式獎勵。圖為蔡總統昨出席總統府前元旦升旗典禮。（記者張嘉明攝） ''' translation_result = translate_client.translate(text_to_translate, target_language='en') print translation_result['translatedText'] ```

issue_title: blob.upload_from_filename blocks for upwards of an hour.
issue_body:
I am using the following code to upload files to GCS.

```python
    blob = bucket.blob(gcs_filepath)
    logging.info('Uploading file "%s" to "%s".', filepath, gcs_filepath)
    blob.upload_from_filename(filepath)
    logging.info('Uploaded file "%s" to "%s".', filepath, gcs_filepath)
```

This works most of the time. However, it has been stuck uploading for the last hour and has not raised an exception yet. It seems like this method should have a timeout or something for situations like this. It would also be helpful if it had the option to log the progress of the method for further diagnosis.

issue_title: WARNING  unsupported addrtype 17, maybe wrong password or encryption method
issue_body:
It seems like a big problem.
When I'm trying to access Gmail, I can't get any response (I can access google though), so I checked the log on shadowsocks server, I found that there are lots of, millions of I mean, erros and warnings.
Typically, it looks like this:

`WARNING  unsupported addrtype 17, maybe wrong password or encryption method`
`ERROR    can not parse header when handling connection from [some IP:some port]`

Sometimes, this IP and this port is the client's IP and port, but sometimes this IP could be a random one from China, France or someplace else on this little planet.

One thing which is very strange is the ERROR and WARNING logs always come together with the INFO, which means it's not likely caused by other users since there is only one user who is me. And every time I initialize an HTTP request, there will be one more WARNING and one more ERROR.

It's funny.
Have anybody else encountered with the same problem?

issue_title: Option mappings in templates for Troposphere like Pyplates.
issue_body:
Hello,

I originally started out using Pyplates and it has an awesome feature.  That feature is a command line argument to specify an options mapping .yaml file.  This let me setup an Integration, QA, and Production environment using one Python template.  The problem is the Pyplates Github repo seems dead and an issue on DependsOn statements I opened will probably never be looked at.  I'd rather not use a tool with little to no current development so, I'm abandoning the idea of Pyplates.

This leaves me wanting to use Troposphere to convert my CF to PY and then use Troposphere py templates as needed to generate an environment options mapped CF template.  I'd love to have a Python wrapper that allows specifying options mappings.  Is there something else I'm missing to quickly set this up or should I bite the bullet and try writing my own script to generate templates by feeding the Troposphere .py file variables from my own script?

issue_title: upgrade pex to a modern version of setuptools
issue_body: newer versions of setuptools have changed pretty dramatically.  pkg_resources is now a package and version identifiers adhere to PEP440.  this should probably coincide with pex 1.0 as it's a major backwards incompatible change.

issue_title: Release 1.1.8
issue_body: a breakfix release to address https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex/issues/260

issue_title: Problem sharing the artifact cache between two different users
issue_body:
I'm seeing this problem when trying to setup my laptop as a  read-only client of an artifact cache populated in our CI builder.  Our CI builder runs under the username `kochiku-user`:

```
$  ./pants compile foo
17:11:59 00:00 [main]
               See a report at: http://localhost:59407/run/pants_run_2015_07_07_17_11_59_699
17:11:59 00:00   [bootstrap]
17:12:01 00:02   [setup]
17:12:01 00:02     [parse]
               Executing tasks in goals: bootstrap -> imports -> unpack-jars -> deferred-sources -> gen -> resolve -> compile
17:12:02 00:03   [bootstrap]
17:12:02 00:03     [bootstrap-jvm-tools]
17:12:02 00:03   [imports]
17:12:02 00:03     [ivy-imports]
17:12:04 00:05       [cache]        
                   No cached artifacts for 8 targets.
                   Invalidated 8 targets.
                   Mapping import jars for external-protos/google/src/main/proto:proto-source-set: 
                     jar('com.google.protobuf', 'protobuf-java', '2.6.1.square.1.2')
17:12:04 00:05       [map-jars]
                     ==== stderr ====
                     
                     ==== stdout ====
                     
17:12:14 00:15   [complete]
               FAILURE

Exception message: Failed to read ng output after 10 seconds.
 jvm_options=['-Duser.timezone=UTC', '-Xmx1g', '-XX:MaxPermSize=256m', u'-Dsun.io.useCanonCaches=false', u'-Divy.cache.dir=/Users/zundel/.ivy2/pants'] classpath=['/data/app/kochiku-worker/.ivy2/pants/com.martiansoftware/nailgun-server/jars/nailgun-server-0.9.1.jar', '/Users/zundel/.ivy2/pants/org.apache.ivy/ivy/jars/ivy-2.3.0.jar']
```

`/data/app/kochiku-worker/.ivy2/pants/com.martiansoftware/nailgun-server/jars/nailgun-server-0.9.1.jar` is a file that only exists on our CI 

Only Square uses the ivy-import task, but this just happens to be the first time we try to invoke a nailgun server.  

Turning off the cache in the jvm_bootstrapper should fix the problem.  I think that would be:

```
[cache.bootstrap.bootstrap-jvm-tools]
read: False
```

issue_title: The @CmdLine apt processor should conform to the new javac compiler plugin spec for resource file outputs
issue_body:
This was originally filed here: https://github.com/twitter/commons/issues/208

See https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/issues/132

Add this support to https://github.com/twitter/commons/blob/master/src/java/com/twitter/common/args/apt/CmdLineProcessor.java

issue_title: Enable implicit_sources by default
issue_body:
The `target-arguments.implicit_sources` option is extremely helpful in encouraging good target hygiene. We should enable it by default, after a period in which we warn if it is disabled.

To avoid being too noisy with the warning, it should be possible to conditionally warn only if implicit sources is False, and implicit sources _would_ be applied for a target... ie: warn in locations where we would apply the option, despite it being disabled. Then, after a release cycle or two, we can invert the default for the option and deprecate it.

issue_title: Update target scopes docs
issue_body:
Target scopes are a great way to influence which classpath a dependency chain is added to. This is documented at http://pantsbuild.github.io/jvm_projects.html#target-scopes

The docs should be updated to reference the `test` scope, as well as mentioning multiple scopes can be added in a space-separated list. This is useful, for example, if you want to mimic the maven `provided` scope, which in pants would be `scope='compile test'`.

issue_title: "pants changed" should fail but does not on changed invalid BUILD file
issue_body:
To repro:

```
echo // >> BUILD
./pants changed

```
Expectation is it should fail in parsing the changed BUILD file, which is does in 0.0.69. But in 0.0.70 this passes silently.

issue_title: Invalidating local cache artifacts
issue_body:
If a bad, build breaking, artifact is inserted into the restful build cache and copied to the local cache it is hard to get it removed.
* We can remove the artifact from the server, but it will still exist in developers local cache.
* We can ask all developers to clean their local cache. Not ideal.
* We can do a no-op change to a source file in the target, but that requires the developer to pull the latest source.

None of these are great solutions. Any ideas on how to solve this issue?

issue_title: add_picture corruptes file in some case
issue_body:
Dear @scanny ,

First of all, thanks to you and all contributors for python-docx!

I have found a problem when I wanted to add images in an existing file. After the insertion the file resulted corrupted when opening it with MS Word.

I did some debugging I have found out that it was a clash between the id of the wp:docPr of the newly inserted image and the wp:docPr of a shape in the header.

I think that the problem is in next_id(self) of document.py that from my understanding looks only for the image ids in document.xml and not in header1.xml (where the conflicting wp:docPr is).
I have temporarily fixed it for my case by artificially increasing the id number of the newly inserted pictures by 10000 as I haven't find out as to access the header1.xml elements and I'm very short on time.

Again, thanks for this extremely useful package!

Support for frozen apps
issue_body:
We would like imageio to work well in frozen apps.

For now, frozen apps with imageio should just work, but required binaries are still downloaded at runtime. This requires an internet connection, so it may be good to provide a way to _ship_ the binary dependencies.

For this we need to provide a way for the developer to copy all required binaries to the app directory (so they can be shipped along) and we need to _look_ in that directory too.

issue_title: Adapting parserator to handle an entire document
issue_body:
I am currently using Parserator to parse short strings, like this: 

> s of 1110 of an hour. The maximum amount to be paid under this contract is $20,000.00. No amount of > work is guaranteed under this agreement; payments wil

and this 

> General Liability insurance will be purchased and maintained with limits of $1,000,000 per occurrence > an

I extract these strings using a loose regular expression ".{75}\$[0-9]+.{75}" on documents that are usually 5 to 10 pages long. I'm most interested in tagging and categorizing the dollar values. Often, the 100 characters around the string is enough to categorize the dollar value. But in some cases I need input from other parts of the document to do the tagging (ex. earlier in the document it might mention that the document is a lease).

@fgregg has pointed me here to show how you could do this with crfsuite http://nbviewer.ipython.org/github/tpeng/python-crfsuite/blob/master/examples/CoNLL%202002.ipynb but I am wondering if it might be possible with the parserator wrapper. All uninteresting tokens would be tagged as <skip> and the interesting ones would be tagged with their proper values. 

I wanted to see what you all thought about (1) using parserator in this way (2) adapting parserator to cover such cases. The biggest hold up to using parserator in this way is tagging documents with hundreds and hundreds of tokens. It seems like you would want a small document annotation GUI to generate the XML to train parserator. Do you think that such a GUI should be part of the library? Do you think this would work? Would you be open to a pull request?

issue_title: Handle Clergy Prefixes
issue_body:
The parser does not handle the "Rev." (short for Reverend) prefix correct: 

"Bob Smith" => [('Rev.', 'GivenName'), ('Bob', 'MiddleName'), ('Smith', 'Surname')]

"Rev. Bob & Susan Smith" => [('Rev.', 'And'), ('Bob', 'GivenName'), ('&', 'And'), ('Susan', 'GivenName'), ('Smith', 'Surname')]

"Rev. Bob & Susan Sugar" => [('Rev.', 'CorporationName'), ('Bob', 'CorporationName'), ('&', 'CorporationName'), ('Susan', 'CorporationName'), ('Sugar', 'CorporationName')]

I think "Rev." should be classified as a "PrefixOther"

issue_title: Possible python 2.7 support
issue_body:
Would you please consider making this code python 2.7 compatible? 

Our code base is large and using python 2.7, and it's not possible for us to migrate to 3.x any time soon. Considering your old API is going to be obsolete by July, it would be great if this API was backward (python 2.7) compatible. 

At a glance, I can see one main import `import http.client` which would prevent it from being backward compatible, and it has a relatively easy solution:

1) Adding a pip requirement for `future` library 
2) Adding these two lines at the top of iyzico_resource.py 
```
from future import standard_library
standard_library.install_aliases()
```

I'll check and try to spot other compatibility issues. 

Please let us know what you think.

issue_title: Apple Music using #AlbumView causes crashes
issue_body:
Apple Music has a number of issues with Sonos, and this one seems to also affect the Windows Desktop Controller. 

When you Queue up an entire album, Soco fails on the first track change with an unknown UPNP type of object.container.album.musicAlbum.#AlbumView

Since I convert everything down to regular dicts in my app, it's very frustrating every time a new service is added which does something new.  Incidentally, the use of #AlbumView is even breaking the Windows controller, causing the player to stop and the queue to jump to the first track and loop the first 1-2 seconds over and over.  So this is clearly not just a Soco issue but rather a larger implementation issue with Sonos and Didl.

I'm assuming this has the same attributes as musicAlbum, so I monkeypatched data_structures with this which seems to resolve it for me, but I'm not sure if there are better ways to handle the continously changing Didl datastructures.  

class DidlMusicAlbumView(DidlAlbum):

    """Class that represents a music library album. """

    item_class = 'object.container.album.musicAlbum.#AlbumView'
    # According to the spec, all musicAlbums should be represented in
    # XML by a <container> tag. Sonos sometimes uses <container> and
    # sometimes uses <item>. Set the tag type to '' to indicate that
    # either is allowed.
    tag = ''
    # name: (ns, tag)
    # pylint: disable=protected-access
    _translation = DidlAudioItem._translation.copy()
    _translation.update(
        {
            'artist': ('upnp', 'artist'),
            'genre': ('upnp', 'genre'),
            'producer': ('upnp', 'producer'),
            'toc': ('upnp', 'toc'),
            'album_art_uri': ('upnp', 'albumArtURI'),
        }
    )
